ddls:
CREATE TABLE "storage_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storage device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the storage device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the storage device (e.g., HDD, SSD, USB) */,
  "total_capacity" TEXT /* Total storage capacity of the device */,
  "total_lbas" INTEGER /* Total number of logical block addresses (LBAs) on the device */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the storage device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the storage device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the storage device */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date the device was purchased */,
  "warranty_expiration" TEXT /* Date the warranty for the device expires */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, decommissioned, in maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_storage_devices_device_type FOREIGN KEY ("device_type") REFERENCES device_types ("device_type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "partitions" (
  "partition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each partition */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the storage device the partition belongs to */,
  "partition_number" INTEGER /* Number of the partition on the device */,
  "total_sectors" INTEGER /* Total number of sectors in the partition */,
  "sys_area_sectors" INTEGER /* Number of sectors allocated to the system area */,
  "user_data_sectors" INTEGER /* Number of sectors allocated to user data */,
  "user_data_bytes" INTEGER /* Number of bytes allocated to user data */,
  "partition_type" TEXT /* Type of partition (e.g., primary, extended, logical) */,
  "file_system" TEXT /* File system used in the partition (e.g., ext4, NTFS, FAT32) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the partition (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Last modification date of the partition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("partition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_partitions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES storage_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "device_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "device_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  "max_capacity" TEXT /* Maximum capacity for the device type */,
  "average_read_speed" REAL /* Average read speed for the device type */,
  "average_write_speed" REAL /* Average write speed for the device type */,
  "typical_use_case" TEXT /* Common use cases for the device type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "partition_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "partition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the partition that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., resize, format) */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the changed attribute */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the changed attribute */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the change */,
  "old_file_system" TEXT /* Old file system type before the change */,
  "new_file_system" TEXT /* New file system type after the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_partition_logs_partition_id FOREIGN KEY ("partition_id") REFERENCES partitions ("partition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_partition_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, IT staff, analyst) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, modify, delete) */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., device, partition) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the storage device */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., read/write speed, IOPS) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric collection */,
  "read_latency" REAL /* Read latency for the device */,
  "write_latency" REAL /* Write latency for the device */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate for the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_metrics_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES storage_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "data_center_name" TEXT /* Name of the data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data center */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data center */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Name of the manager responsible for the data center */,
  "manager_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the data center manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_data_center" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the storage device */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data center */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed in the data center */,
  "decommission_date" TEXT /* Date the device was decommissioned from the data center */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device in the data center (e.g., active, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_data_center_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES storage_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_data_center_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)
